questions.text: Name the body part we use to see.
Name one part of a plant that grows under the ground.
What should we do before eating food?
Write two sentences about your family.
questions.answer: Example: My family has my parents, my sister and me. We love and help each other.

questions.text: Why are plants important to us? Write two reasons.
questions.answer: Plants give us food to eat and fresh air (oxygen) to breathe. They also give shade and beauty.

questions.text:
Assertion (A): We should brush our teeth every day.
Reason (R): Brushing keeps our teeth clean and healthy.
options: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Brushing daily keeps teeth clean and healthy — R correctly explains A.

questions.text:
Assertion (A): Fish live in water.
Reason (R): Fish breathe using gills.
options: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Fish have gills to breathe in water, so they live in water. Both true and related.

questions.text: Match the animal with its home.
Match the body part with its use.
We should waste water while brushing.
questions.explanation: We should save water, not waste it. False.
Yes, a hen lays eggs. True.
